blocks.content: The worst claw machine in the building had a broken rotation mechanism.

The claw could only travel forward and back—no left, no right—which Cassandra described as heroically limited and proceeded to lose four tokens to before winning a small yellow chick barely distinguishable from a lemon.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You can only move it in one direction.

They moved through the arcade like that—finding things, failing at things, arguing about technique and luck and whether a machine could be sentient enough to hold a grudge. Cassandra was loud when she lost and quiet when she won, which was somehow the opposite of what he'd expected.
At the skee-ball lane, she asked him to show her his form.
She corrected his elbow, which she knew nothing about and was completely confident about anyway.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I've been playing skee-ball for fifteen years.
He rolled his eyes and let her.
Her hand closed over his forearm to adjust the angle—
Like something had shifted slightly in the room.
It went in the center ring.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I wasn't going to say anything.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I was absolutely going to say something.
And so, without quite meaning to, was he.
